narratives.name: AI Infrastructure Buildout
narratives.thesis: Demand for AI chips and related infrastructure continues to drive strong performance in semiconductor companies and hardware providers, as hyperscalers and enterprises invest heavily.

NVDA revenue growth slows below 20% QoQ
China export controls expand to HBM

narratives.name: GLP-1 Obesity Wave
narratives.thesis: The strong demand for GLP-1 obesity and diabetes drugs continues to drive significant growth and positive sentiment for leading pharmaceutical companies.

Unexpected safety concerns arise for existing GLP-1 drugs

narratives.name: China AI Self-Sufficiency Push
narratives.thesis: China's efforts to develop its own AI chip ecosystem due to export controls are creating headwinds for US chipmakers reliant on the Chinese market, while also leading to capital burn for Chinese tech giants.
watchFor: US imposes new, broader export controls on chip manufacturing equipment
China announces a breakthrough in domestic advanced chip production
Chinese tech giants report significant slowdown in AI investment

Technology.thesis: The AI capex cycle continues to be the dominant driver, with hyperscalers and chipmakers investing heavily. Software companies are also seeing a boost from AI integration, though some concerns about valuation and China's self-sufficiency are emerging.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings – would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM – forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging – would hit chip supply chain

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: The sector is showing resilience in some areas like discount retail and residential construction, but specialty retail and auto manufacturers face headwinds from consumer sentiment and valuation concerns. Travel services are seeing a rebound.
watchFor: Unexpectedly hot CPI print > 0.4% MoM – would signal consumer spending pressure
Consumer confidence index drops significantly below 95
Interest rates rise, impacting mortgage demand and auto loans

Communication Services.thesis: Internet content and information companies are benefiting from AI integration and user growth, but entertainment faces challenges from market comparisons and broader economic concerns. Chinese internet stocks remain under pressure.
watchFor: Major tech platforms report slowing ad revenue growth
New regulations on AI content generation impact user engagement
Increased competition in streaming services leads to subscriber churn

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense is benefiting from geopolitical tensions, while Farm & Heavy Construction Machinery shows strengthening growth drivers. Integrated Freight & Logistics faces valuation concerns and mixed sentiment.
watchFor: De-escalation of major geopolitical conflicts
Global economic slowdown impacts industrial demand
Rising fuel costs significantly impact logistics companies

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive strong performance for leaders, but the broader healthcare sector faces mixed signals with some medical device companies experiencing headwinds and healthcare plans under scrutiny.
watchFor: New GLP-1 competitors emerge with superior efficacy or fewer side effects
Regulatory changes impact drug pricing or reimbursement policies
Healthcare spending growth slows down significantly

Financials.thesis: The financial sector is generally bullish, driven by strong performance in diversified banks, credit services, and asset management, despite some individual stock volatility.
watchFor: Unexpected interest rate cuts by the Fed – would compress net interest margins
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
Major regulatory changes impacting bank profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: Discount stores are showing strong performance and value propositions, benefiting from consumer focus on affordability. Household & Personal Products are facing headwinds.
watchFor: Significant improvement in consumer discretionary spending, shifting away from discount stores
Increased competition from online retailers in the discount segment
Unexpectedly high inflation impacts input costs for consumer staples

Utilities.thesis: The utilities sector is seeing mixed signals, with some independent power producers facing short-term pullbacks despite long-term growth drivers, and nuclear energy gaining attention as a potential AI energy solution.
watchFor: Significant changes in energy policy or subsidies for renewables/nuclear
Unexpected fluctuations in natural gas or electricity prices
Interest rates rise sharply, increasing borrowing costs for capital-intensive utilities

Basic Materials.thesis: Rare earth stocks are gaining attention as a 'multi-year investment cycle' begins, driven by strategic importance and early-mover advantages.
watchFor: New discoveries of rare earth deposits outside of current dominant regions
Technological advancements reduce reliance on rare earth elements
Geopolitical tensions ease, reducing the strategic importance of rare earths

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is seen as a defensive play against overvalued tech and market volatility, while silver faces pressure from weakening demand.
watchFor: Strong economic data reduces safe-haven demand for precious metals
Significant strengthening of the US Dollar
Central banks reduce gold purchases
